title: dalea lutea var. gigantea
intro: Dalea lutea var. gigantea, also known as the giant prairie clover, is a perennial legume native to the southern Great Plains region of North America. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, due to the presence of glycosides and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as to support digestive health and reduce inflammation. Dalea lutea var. gigantea can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and it is also sometimes used in tinctures and topical applications for its medicinal benefits.
